title: Marlborough on Budget
meta_desc: Discover how to visit Marlborough & Blenheim on a family budget of $180-300/day, with tips on accommodation, activities, and saving money for a fun-filled holiday
intro: Marlborough is a stunning region in the South Island, offering a mix of outdoor activities, beautiful scenery, and family-friendly fun. With a daily budget of $180-300 for a family of four, you can enjoy the best of Marlborough without breaking the bank. From affordable accommodation to free activities, we'll show you how to make the most of your trip.

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body:
When it comes to accommodation in Marlborough, you have a range of options to suit your budget. For a cheap and cheerful stay, consider camping at Momorangi Bay ($20-30 per night) or a hostel in Picton ($80-120 per night). For a mid-range option, look at motels in Blenheim ($150-250 per night) or holiday homes in the Sounds ($200-350 per night).

If you're looking to save money, consider self-catering options like apartments or holiday homes, which can be more cost-effective than hotels or resorts.
list: Camping at Momorangi Bay ($20-30 per night)
Holiday home in the Sounds ($200-350 per night)

sections.heading: Free Activities
sections.body:
Marlborough has plenty of free activities to enjoy, from the beautiful Picton foreshore and Waitohi Picton play area to the stunning Pelorus Bridge Scenic Reserve. You can also visit Momorangi Bay for a free day visit, which is perfect for swimming and picnicking.

Other free activities include visiting the Picton township and exploring the Queen Charlotte Track, which has some family-friendly sections.
list: Picton foreshore and Waitohi Picton play area
Picton township and Queen Charlotte Track

sections.heading: Budget Breakdown
sections.body: To give you a rough idea of how to budget for your trip, here's a breakdown of daily costs for a family of four:

sections.heading: Where to Save and Splurge
sections.body:
To make the most of your budget, consider saving money on accommodation and food by opting for self-catering options and cooking your own meals.

However, there are some activities that are worth splashing out on, such as a dolphin watch cruise ($100-150 per person) or a guided sea kayaking trip ($120-180 per person).
list: Save money on accommodation by opting for self-catering options
Save money on food by cooking your own meals
Splurge on a dolphin watch cruise ($100-150 per person)
Splurge on a guided sea kayaking trip ($120-180 per person)
